description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each displaying a pronounced wood grain texture and natural color variations. Two metallic hoops, likely made of steel or iron, encircle the barrel horizontally—one near the top and one near the bottom—providing structural reinforcement. The top surface is flat and appears to be sealed, with visible wood rings and a faint circular pattern, suggesting it is closed. One side of the barrel features a subtle stamped marking or logo, adding a touch of authenticity and possible identification. The overall finish is matte, with a slightly rough tactile quality typical of untreated or lightly finished wood.
description: A medium wooden barrel with metal hoops, wood grain texture, and a sealed top. Features a stamped marking on one side and a matte, slightly rough finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wood grain, metal hoops, and the sealed top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stamped marking or logo and the curvature of the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible wood texture and metal hoops, no markings present.
description_view_3: Rear view showing uninterrupted wood grain and both metal hoops, with no visible markings.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: stamped marking or logo on one side
